claimBoundary.nonCommunityPolicy: This roadmap compares framework product and engineering capability only; community size, GitHub stars, downloads, contributor count, and brand awareness remain excluded from prioritization.
claimBoundary.rpcPromotionPolicy: RPC latency remains report-only until repeatable trend evidence supports promotion. P9 focuses on release-train evidence, promotion blockers, rollback triggers, and coexistence paths before claiming Tier 1 parity.
claimBoundary.fixtureTrustPolicy: Generated-project compatibility claims require old, current, and future inputs to generate temporary projects, compile or test, repeat without unexplained diffs, and produce a readable diff report.
claimBoundary.liveProofPolicy: Docker-backed reference app and cloud-native checks must prefer real tool execution when dependencies exist; fallback evidence must be explicit, machine-readable, and unsuitable for production parity claims.
claimBoundary.publishProtocolPolicy: Plugin registry publishing requires manifest, digest, signature or trust source, source allowlist, compatibility, permission, malicious-path, and no-partial-write evidence before adopter-facing promotion.

dimensions.gap: Kitex and gRPC-Go still provide deeper transport and release confidence. gofly needs release-train evidence that makes unary and streaming promotion blockers explicit without overstating latency parity.
dimensions.todo: Add release-train RPC evidence for unary, server-stream, client-stream, bidi-stream, resolver and balancer contracts, deadline, retry, auth, tracing, benchmark budget status, Kitex rollback path, and promotion blocker inventory.

dimensions.gap: goctl and Kratos scaffolds are trusted because historical inputs continue to generate, compile, and produce explainable diffs. gofly should move beyond current-profile smoke evidence.
dimensions.todo: Create old, current, and future fixtures for .api, .proto, config, plugin profile, and generated snapshots; generate temporary projects, run compile or tests, repeat generation, and emit explainable diff evidence.

dimensions.gap: Production-ready frameworks provide runnable proof across storage, cache, messaging, discovery, and observability. gofly's production-orders topology still needs stronger live integration evidence.
dimensions.todo: Add Docker-backed live proof rows for SQL outbox, Redis cache, Kafka, RabbitMQ, Redis Stream, Consul, etcd, Nacos, and OpenTelemetry collector, with memory-mode fallback and explicit skip evidence when tools are unavailable.

dimensions.gap: R8 made performance ratchets explicit, but gateway proxy and cache hot paths still need ownership instead of unsupported report-only rows.
dimensions.todo: Add gateway proxy and cache hot path benchmark ownership, five-run baseline, current trend, allocation budget, latency report-only status, and rollback notes.

dimensions.gap: Local release evidence exists, but mature adopters expect hosted tag CI to upload artifact evidence and reject skipped artifact gates.
dimensions.todo: Map hosted tag workflow evidence for artifact upload, SBOM, checksums, provenance, Docker digest, Trivy, CodeQL, Scorecard, Dependency Review, and required-check drift.

dimensions.gap: Cloud-native assets are more credible when Helm, Kustomize, ServiceMonitor, HPA, PDB, and NetworkPolicy render through real tools before fallback evidence is accepted.
dimensions.todo: Add Helm values profiles, rendered golden YAML, kubeconform or kubeval optional report, Kustomize overlays, and explicit fallback reason schema for missing tools.

dimensions.gap: Plugin examples are not enough for a publishable ecosystem. The registry contract must encode compatibility, trust source, digest pinning, source allowlist, and rollback evidence.
dimensions.todo: Harden plugin registry publish protocol with compatibility policy, signature trust source, digest pinning, source allowlist, template contract versions, permission boundaries, malicious path checks, and no partial writes.

dimensions.gap: Framework selection guidance becomes actionable only when each migration path has runnable examples, rollback notes, compatibility caveats, gate commands, and boundary explanations.
dimensions.todo: Expand Gin, go-zero, Kratos, and Kitex migration proof with runnable examples, smoke commands, rollback notes, compatibility caveats, performance boundaries, and governance boundary notes.

dimensions.gap: Mature CLI tools tell adopters what failed and what to do next. gofly should connect doctor, release check, generated failure reports, support bundles, and aiflow handoff actions.
dimensions.todo: Add remediation loop evidence for gofly doctor --json, gofly release check --json, generated project failure report, support bundle, CLI JSON golden fields, and aiflow handoff nextActions.

dimensions.gap: Each P9 task needs committed task, gate, known-risk, ignored-runtime-path, and follow-up evidence so the batch can be audited without local runtime state.
dimensions.todo: Record P9 convergence with per-task commit ids, gate evidence, skipped or downgraded checks, runtime ignored paths, known risks, and next-round recommendations.
